query: Use /automake in this repo to improve the README onboarding path. Consult me on the Optimizer and independent Evaluator instructions before you run anything.
Invoke the Automake skill for src/slugify.py: one Sol Optimizer, one Terra Evaluator, two iterations max, and keep only evidence-backed improvements.
/automake start the ratchet now with the current repo context; stop as soon as python3 -m unittest -q passes.
Please use Automake as the named workflow to design and approve a Git-backed evaluator-optimizer loop for this CLI reliability goal.
Run /automake now in this new folder even though it has no HEAD yet; protect .env.local, establish the baseline, and fix the failing readiness test.
Activate the automake skill for a one-attempt experiment on this status file, and roll back exactly if the independent judge says NOT_BETTER.
/automake improve this dashboard's load time with five iterations, three consecutive failures, and a real browser journey as evaluation evidence.
I explicitly want the Automake skill here: draft separate optimizer.md and evaluator.md artifacts, show me both approval gates, then ask whether to run.
Start the Automake ratchet immediately on the current committed baseline and record every child launch, verdict, learning, and kept commit.
Call automake for this documentation cleanup; the Evaluator should judge the complete artifact for accuracy and audience usefulness, not simulate E2E.
This old C project uses GNU Automake. Update configure.ac and Makefile.am so make distcheck includes the new parser sources.
Add an automake target to our Makefile that regenerates fixtures before the integration tests run.
Fix normalize_slug so repeated underscores collapse, run the unit tests, and commit the change once they pass.
Review this pull request's optimizer and evaluator classes for bugs and explain whether the separation of concerns is sound; do not edit it.
Explain the evaluator-optimizer pattern and give me a small pseudocode example of a ratchet loop for a blog post.
Reset this branch to the last green commit and restore the two tracked files I accidentally deleted.
Set up an overnight autoresearch loop that repeatedly edits this benchmark and keeps the best score; use the autoloop workflow.
Diagnose why our CI retry job keeps launching the same failed child process, but don't implement a fix yet.
Help me write independent Optimizer and Evaluator prompts for an experiment, but do not invoke Automake or run any agents.
Run the existing test suite, make the smallest fix for the one failure, and stop when everything is green.
